title: Polymer Physics & Hydrogels
desc: Investigating chain dynamics, network topology, and stimuli-responsive behavior in synthetic and biopolymer systems.
details: Our ongoing projects include mapping the swelling kinetics of responsive hydrogels under varying thermal gradients and studying their load-bearing viscoelastic properties over time.

title: Colloidal Self-Assembly
desc: Designing colloidal particles that spontaneously organize into functional structures with emergent optical and mechanical properties.
details: We are currently investigating the phase separation kinetics of binary colloidal mixtures under oscillatory shear, focusing on how external mechanical forces drive crystallization.

title: Active & Living Matter
desc: Understanding the collective motion of motile bacteria, cytoskeletal filaments, and synthetic active particles far from equilibrium.
details: Recent efforts involve analyzing the topological defect dynamics in active nematic films when placed under severe geometric confinement.

title: Liquid Crystals
desc: Probing topological defects, phase transitions, and director field dynamics in thermotropic and lyotropic liquid crystalline systems.
details: Current experiments focus on high-precision thermal analysis of phase transitions using 1 mm unaligned cells, controlled via the Julabo Presto system, coupled with light scattering observations to map bulk fluid volume properties.

title: Interfacial Phenomena
desc: Exploring wetting, capillarity, and emulsion stability at soft interfaces — from Pickering emulsions to biological membranes.
details: We are probing wetting transitions on biomimetic substrates to build a unifying framework for contact angle hysteresis in complex fluid drops.

title: Biophysics & Biomaterials
desc: Bridging soft matter and biology: from extracellular matrix mechanics to the design of biomimetic scaffolds for regenerative medicine.
details: Working closely with biological models to understand how the mechanical stiffness of extracellular matrices influences cell motility and differentiation.
